name: Feel. Experience. Design. - Design thinking workshop
description:
Design Thinking is a human-centred approach to problem-solving - one that starts not with solutions, but with genuine understanding of the people the problem affects. Originally developed in the world of product and service design, it is now used across industries, organisations, and social contexts to tackle everything from workplace culture to public policy.

The workshop takes participants through all five stages of Design Thinking - Empathise, Define, Ideate, Prototype, and Test - using the tools of Applied Theatre. Every stage is experienced in the body, not just understood in the mind.

Who is this for?
Professionals, practitioners, and curious thinkers who work with people, systems, or ideas - and want a deeper, more honest way of understanding the problems they solve. Useful for product managers, HR and L&D professionals, designers, consultants, educators, social sector workers, facilitators, and anyone who has sat through one too many sticky-note sessions and wanted something more real.

No prior knowledge of Design Thinking or theatre required. Curiosity is the only prerequisite.

About the Facilitator
Bejoy Balagopal - Co-founder of First Drop Theatre. Part engineer, part storyteller, long-time theatre practitioner. 25 years navigating corporate and creative worlds. Holds a Professional Certificate in Design Thinking and Innovation with AI from IIM Kozhikode.
Poster Description - Features a warm, textured background with soft circular shapes. The words - "Feel. Experience. Design." - are displayed in large, hand-lettered text, with a photograph of the facilitator in the upper right. A row of small sticky notes are scattered across the middle of the poster, each carrying a single word - Feel, Inhabit, Question, Build, Break, Discover.

name: Natural Dyeing Workshop for Beginners - Art Workshop
description:
Discover the fascinating world of natural colours in this hands-on Natural Dyeing Workshop designed for beginners. Learn how everyday natural materials such as onion peels, marigold flowers, sappan wood, indigo, and other plant-based ingredients can be transformed into beautiful, sustainable dyes for textiles.
The workshop will guide you through the complete dyeing process, starting with an introduction to natural dyes and the importance of fabric preparation. You will learn the fundamentals of mordanting : a crucial step that helps colours bond with the fabric and improves colour fastness. Through step-by-step demonstrations, participants will explore dye extraction, fabric preparation, dyeing techniques, and colour development using a range of natural ingredients.
The session will also introduce dye modification techniques, showing how simple modifiers can alter and expand the colour palette obtained from a single dye source. Participants will gain practical knowledge of sustainable textile practices while experimenting with colours and patterns.
As part of the workshop, you will create your own tie-dyed naturally dyed fabric samples and take them home, along with the confidence to continue exploring natural dyeing techniques on your own.
Duration: 2 hours Suitable for: Beginners, craft enthusiasts, students, designers, and anyone interested in sustainable textile practices.

name: Create Your Own 3D Texture Moon Lamp - Art Workshop
description:
Bring the moon to life through texture, dimension, and light. In this hands-on workshop, participants will sculpt and design their own moon-inspired lamp using texture mediums and artistic techniques to create a stunning three-dimensional surface. Once illuminated, the textured details cast beautiful shadows, turning each piece into a unique work of functional art.
What Participants Will Learn

Fundamentals of 3D texture art
Creating depth and dimension using texture mediums
Surface sculpting and layering techniques
Design composition on curved surfaces
Finishing and detailing techniques for a professional look
Creative expression through texture and form

Takeaway:
A handcrafted illuminated moon lamp featuring a unique textured design, ready to display at home or work.

name: A Pause for Busy Minds - A Screen-Free Creative Reset
description:
When was the last time you spent a couple of hours completely offline, just making something with your hands and laughing with new people?
If your brain is tired of endless screen time and awkward networking, A Pause for Busy Minds is for you. No elevator pitches, no business cards, and absolutely zero "artistic talent" required. Just a cozy space to unplug and play.
 
What to expect:
🎲 Low-stress collaborative games (minus the awkward icebreakers)
📖 Fun, story-driven exercises to get to know people naturally
🎨 Hands-on art activities designed purely to help you unwind
 
Come as you are, put your phone away, and let’s remind ourselves how to connect like humans again.  If nothing else, you will at least make one new connection.

name: Move through Math  - Book launch
description:
This event marks the release of Move Through Math, a handbook designed for parents, facilitators, and educators working with children aged 5–10. Rooted in the principles of embodied education, the book explores how abstract mathematical concepts can be understood through movement, sound, rhythm, and spatial awareness. By adapting movements from Bharatanatyam, the handbook offers a culturally grounded, multisensory approach to STEM learning.
Program Highlights:


Author Presentation: An overview of the educational framework and the philosophy of embodied pedagogy.


Live Demonstration: Selected mathematical concepts expressed through Bharatanatyam-inspired movement.


Interactive Segment: A guided, accessible movement exploration for the audience.


Q&A and Networking: Open discussion followed by a book signing.

name: Sunday Book Club - Book Club
description:
Join Sunday Book Club, a reading circle exploring books on history and global politics. This July, we will read “Daughters of the Bamboo Grove: China's Stolen Children” and the story "Separated Twins" by Barbara Demick.
Age Group: 15 +

name: Atta Galatta presents “Support Group” - Support Group
description:
People make up big cities, but what makes up people’s lives, hopefully not loneliness. Loneliness is an intensely painful state that arises out of a lack of intimacy and connectedness. People from every walk of life, even those with social contacts or pursuing successful careers, can experience sadness from the absence of belonging. This painful experience is felt in a myriad of contexts, such as breakups, disagreeing families, fear of rejection, unrelatability, shyness, and so on
This support group has been constituted to bring people struggling with disconnectedness in various contexts together for a shared experience of comfort, belonging, laughter, guidance, and above all, to break the cycle of sustained loneliness
The support is being organized and moderated by Nimesh George and Gokul R, who are practicing psychologists based in Bangalore

name: Lahe Lahe Caravan presents “Harry Potter Book Discussion” - Book Discussion
description:
For hard-core Potterheads age 12 and above!
Get into the minds of the characters, ferret out the intricacies of the plot - enter the wizarding world and share your thoughts and insights!
Will help if you have read all the Harry Potter books - this discussion will be based only on the books and not the movies.
The event is curated by Lahe Lahe Caravan and is being hosted at Atta Galatta.

name: Dialogue with your Subconscious in the language of ART - No Small Talk. Real Inner Work
description:
Come join me as we set up a dialogue with our inner self. We'll curate a safe, judgment-free space to express & introspect, bypassing conscious wiring to access the subconscious mind. Art makes this possible.
Through this session, you’ll have unexpected insights and mini-epiphanies and leave with a conscious takeaway about your psyche. Bring nothing but intentional presence, because all materials will be provided.
Hosted by Aarushi Saxena:
I used art as a release for more than 20 years, but when I learned to read art and began using it as a means to interpret my intrapsychic landscape, that's when my healing journey really began. And it has been deeply rewarding! My aim is to be a communication facilitator for others to dialogue with their inner self and experience their own psyche breakthroughs. If you're looking for healing, this is an ideal pressure-free medium. Think of this session as a gentle preview of what can unfold over a longer timeline dedicated to spiritual art practices.
What We’ll Do:

Simple Breathwork & Energy Flow: Centering ourselves to prepare the mind.
Lots of ART: Engaging directly with the canvas to let the subconscious speak.
Some Introspection: Looking deeply into the visual language we create.

What You’ll Get:

Bold insights into your subconscious thought-loops, patterns, and limitations
An avenue for long-term spiritual art guidance
Included in your ticket is a private, 1-on-1 session to be set up within 8 weeks post-workshop.

name: Science Café - Free Science Conversations
description:
We are delighted to host the third edition of the Science Cafe conversations on the most intriguing topic - Is there a convergence between history and science? Are they both shaped by facts and evidence? Which scientific innovations have made greatest historic impact on human civilization? 
We will be transgressing fine margins of history and science to introspect how we ended up in such chaotic uncertain times? 
Do join us for a wonderful evening of freewheeling deep conversations on 5th July!

name: Down by the Mangroves - A fun book reading
description:
About the book:
Meet Moyna, a fifth grader whose backyard is the vast, breathing Sundarbans — where herons stand still as secrets, kingfishers flash over the water, and the legend of Bonbibi, guardian of the forest, lives on. But when her father is suddenly gone, the world she knew begins to quietly shift. 
Down by the Mangroves is a tender story about loving your world, watching it change and discovering that sorrow and joy can share the same sky.
About the authors, Sheela and Praba: 
Sheela and Praba are storybrewers who love tales of every kind—big, small, wild, and gently hopeful. Writing across oceans and time zones, they craft stories where young readers can find a friend, feel seen, and remember that they’re not alone.
Their previous picture book, Thukpa for All, has been recognized by the International Board on Books for Young People (IBBY) and the South Asia Book Award, and featured on the Bank Street Best Children’s Books list.
About Praba Ram: 
Praba is a children's writer, SAT coach, and a college essay mentor in Chennai, Tamil Nadu. She has written over 14 children's books with eclectic themes drawn from every thread of India - from a feline exploring shapes, to a boy on the banks of the Brahmaputra learning about nonviolent silk and river dolphins, to Maldharis of the Gir Forest, to thukpa for all celebrating Tibetan culture and featuring Ladakhi landscapes. Stories that showcase the uncommon, unsung themes are her passion.

name: Zon Within - Intuitive Tarot Workshop
description:
Over the last three years, I have explored, wandered, and searched for myself. In the process, I left the corporate world in Bangalore, moved from the misty mountains of Bir in Himachal to the relentless joy of the ocean in Goa, my second home. I learnt, unlearnt, questioned almost everything, and slowly found my way back to myself.
While navigating the uncertainty of building something of my own, I found an anchor in Tarot. I didn't go looking for it, but it stayed because it helped me hear myself. Not through prediction, but through reflection. It became the tool I reached for whenever I needed clarity on what I was truly feeling and where I wanted to go.
Now, I'm bringing a part of that journey to Bangalore.
I'd love to invite you to an intimate evening where I'll share the origins of Tarot, how it can be used as a tool for intuition, and guide you through your first experience with the cards. My hope is that you leave with a practice you can return to whenever life feels a little noisy.
Join me for an evening of reflection, introspection, and reconnecting with yourself. No prior knowledge is needed, only curiosity and a willingness to listen to your inner wisdom.
📅 Friday, 10th July · 5:30–8:00 PM
📍 Atta Galatta, Indiranagar, Bangalore
🎟️ ₹1,800 · Limited seats
🔗 https://forms.gle/e9uK36Crdx8pYWud

name: Qu for Quiz - Quiz
description: It's Friday and it's QuforQuiz. So say tata to office and be at Atta Galatta.

name: Plaeto Shoe Painting Workshop - Painting workshop by Plaeto
description:
Plaeto's Sole Art Junior is a creative workshop designed for young artists and their parents to explore art together. Through hands-on activities, kids unleash their imagination and craft unique designs in a warm, playful setting. A celebration of creativity, self-expression, and the joy of making something truly one's own.
Age Group: 04-14 years

name: Fat2Fit – Intimate Reading Session - Book Reading
description:
Over five years ago, I stood in front of a mirror at 110 kilos and made a promise to myself. That promise changed my life.
Fat2Fit is not a book about weight loss. It is a story about resilience, self-respect, healing, second chances, and discovering what becomes possible when we refuse to give up on ourselves.
I would love for you to join me for an intimate reading and conversation as I share stories from the book, the lessons behind the journey, and reflections that never made it onto social media.

name: How to Choose the Right Mutual Fund - Stop Guessing. Start Investing for Your Real-Life Goals
description:
Do you feel confused and overwhelmed when thinking about mutual funds? With thousands of options and endless financial jargon, just trying to pick the right fund can feel completely exhausting. Most people end up stuck in analysis paralysis or chasing past returns, hoping for the best.
It’s time to stop guessing.
This 11th July, join us at Atta Galatta, Indiranagar, for a completely hands-on, thinking workshop designed to clear the noise. We aren’t here to deliver dry lectures or bombard you with complex charts. Instead, this is a practical, exercise-driven session where you will actively decode the basics of mutual funds and learn exactly how to align them with your real-life milestones.
Whether you are saving for a short-term break or mapping out long-term wealth, you will learn the exact framework needed to filter and select the right funds for your personal timeline. You won't just sit and listen—you will actively build your own investment roadmap right in the room.
Leave the confusion behind and walk out with absolute clarity and a concrete action plan for your money. Seats are strictly limited to ensure personal attention during the exercises. Secure your spot today!

name: Atta Galatta Presents "AG Book CLub" - Book Club
description:
What have you been reading? Come, discuss your latest read. Each member gets 5-7 minutes to talk about the book, theme, plot, with 2 minutes to answer any questions others might have. This month’s special focus will be
Books in a series- come tell us what you've been reading.
June’s special focus will be on Humour

name: The Almost Perfects - Book launch
description:
This will be a debut novel launch of 13-year-old Ksheeraja MD, who began storytelling at an extremely young age. Her experiences have moulded her to pen down a novel on long-lasting friendship. 
The event will be chaired by Smt Prapulla, who is a technical writer, knowledge seeker and herself an author of "Many Shades of Marriage," co-authored with Dr Mythri Ramachandra. 
Vajrashri Anand, who gracefully wrote the novel's foreword, will be moderating the event. 
The event will comprise an introduction to the chief guest, the author and the book, followed by a book reading, audience interaction and finally the distribution of signed author copies 
About the Book:
Not every story is about toppers, gold medalists, or those kids who seem to know all the answers before the teacher even asks the question. This one is about the other side of the classroom-the ones who make mistakes, laugh too loud, and somehow turn detention into the best part of the week.
This book isn't about perfection. It's about being almost perfect-which, honestly, is way more fun, because in between low grades, prank disasters, and embarrassing moments, there are friendships that make the chaos worth it. If you've ever been a dreamer or someone who laughed in the middle of silence, this story is for you.

name: Ghost of Garrincha - Paperclip Live - Storytelling
description:
The Paperclip is an independent media platform widely followed on social media for its deeply researched, authentic storytelling in an era of viral misinformation. Known for turning complex histories into compelling narratives, it now brings its most powerful football stories to a live audience for the first time in the city.
With the FIFA World Cup going on, this show taps into a moment when football is already on everyone’s mind - but goes far beyond the game. It speaks to an urban audience that is globally aware, engaged with geopolitics, and curious about the world beyond headlines. Blending football with power, politics, and human stories, the experience is designed to be highly engaging - even for those who don’t follow the sport. It’s not just a show for fans, but for anyone who enjoys sharp, thought-provoking storytelling.

name: Dead on Arrival: A Play in Silent Acts - Play
description:
Some things said at a party follow you home.
Some silences last eight years.
Some conversations arrive too late.
 
Dead on Arrival is a play about what happens inside a long marriage when two people stop hearing each other - not all at once, but slowly, in the ordinary moments no one warns you about.
Written by Akaash Jayaram, this is not a story about a dramatic collapse. It is about the quieter kind, the kind that looks, from the outside, like everything is fine.
 
What You Will Watch:
You will enter one night in Bengaluru. A party. A bedroom. A conversation that took so long to happen.
You will hear what he said, what she heard, and what neither of them said at all.
You will meet Minu, not at a moment of crisis, but at the moment just before one. The moment when a person stops waiting for a better time and decides to make one.
This is a play about the words we say and the ones we never take back.
 
Why MDG Is Bringing You This:
At MDG, we work with people navigating separation and divorce every day. What we have observed is this: most transitions do not begin with a legal filing. They begin with a feeling that a voice has gone silent but no one is ready to address it.
Dead on Arrival puts that feeling on stage. It names it. And in doing so, it makes space for conversations that many people have never been able to start.
This is not therapy. It is theatre. But it may do what the best theatre does - show you something true about yourself.

name: Abstract Art Workshop  - Art Workshop
description:
Hi, I'm Thomas, an artist, content creator, and graphic designer. I'm hosting an art workshop at Atta Galatta on the 11th of July. The workshop will be a 2 hour secession, that's going to be similar to a crash course on the basic as well as the advanced techniques of abstract art.
 
In the workshop, I will teach you some of the important avenues that would help you monetize your artwork as well. You will also understand just how therapeutic and calming this art form really is.
 
Key pointers on pricing, how you could turn your art into merchandise, and how to go about the framing and sales aspects will also be covered during the course of the 2 hours.

name: Queer Quiet Hours - Meetup
description:
Queer Quiet Hours returns for July 2026!
Register here - http://bit.ly/adhdqueeple
 
A soft little corner for LGBTQIA+ folks to slow down, make art, read, journal, daydream, or simply exist in shared company.
 
There’s no pressure to interact. Come with your own project, or just arrive as you are. We’ll also have art materials around for anyone who wants to use them.
 
✨ This space is for LGBTQIA+ folks only. We haven’t opened it up to allies yet. Thank you for understanding.
 
🆓 Free to join, but spots are limited. Link in bio.
 
📅  12 JULY, 2026
⏰ Sundays, 2 - 5 PM
📍 @attagalatta, Indiranagar, Bangalore
📸 No photos taken without clear consent.

name: Emotional Well- Being for Women - Workshop
description:
Our life journeys are different from the time we are born, so the definition of what emotional well-being means to each one of us ought to be unique to us. There is no one-size-fits-all approach.
In an age where everything we buy is personalised, do you want to know what your emotional well-being looks like? Then, this session is for you. A reflective session curated to help you discover the building blocks of your emotional well-being, your way.
Facilitated by Sandya Jadhav, an Emotional Well-being Life Coach (ICF ACC Practitioner). She empowers women to build emotionally healthy lives that resonate with their true selves.
Age Group: 25 +
Website: www.sathyastudio.com

name: Get in touch with your Body - Body Awareness Workshop
description:
“When was the last time you played without worrying about being good at it?”
As children, play came naturally. We explored, experimented, imagined, and expressed ourselves freely. Somewhere along the way, many of us traded play for productivity, performance, and perfection.
Get In Touch With Your Body is a playful movement and expressive arts workshop that invites you to return to that space of curiosity. Through creative movement, simple art-based activities, and shared experiences, you'll explore what happens when you let go of "doing it right" and allow yourself to simply engage, create, and move.
Play is more than fun. It helps us become more flexible, creative, connected, and present. It opens the door to self-expression, reduces stress, nurtures resilience, and reminds us that growth doesn't always have to be serious.
No dance experience. No art experience. No pressure to perform.
Just bring your curiosity, your willingness to explore, and a little room for play.
Come move, create, connect, and rediscover the joy of being fully alive in your body.

name: Playback Theatre Jam - Interactive theater workshop
description:
How would it be to indulge in an evening of fun, spontaneity, and creativity while getting a taster of a very powerful format of theatre - Playback Theatre.
What is Playback Theatre?
A socio-improv, interactive format of theatre where audience members share real-life moments and feelings, and actors improvise to reflect back the stories, on-the-spot.
What would the evening have?
 A friendly, open, non-judgmental space
 Opportunity to connect with a creative community
 A nudge to think out-of-the-box
The jam is open to everyone - seasoned Playbackers, beginners, people who have never indulged in theatre; basically, anyone curious to experience this unique theatre form and have fun being creative.
How would it be to indulge in an evening of fun, spontaneity, and creativity while getting a taster of a very powerful format of theatre - Playback Theatre.

name: Auto Akka and the Hot Seat - Book reading and workshop
description:
Bangalore is sizzling! And young Ravi’s favourite blue, sticker-covered water bottle is officially missing. Jump into the hot seat with Auto Akka, the city's coolest auto rickshaw driver, for an interactive story reading and creative workshop! Based on the children's storybook Auto Akka and the Hot Seat, this fun reading session will take us all on a fast-paced hunt through bustling streets while exploring the real-world realities of urban heat and climate resilience. After the book reading, children will get hands-on and creative, sketching, designing, and inventing their very own climate resilient, cool autos to beat the heat!
About the Facilitators: 
Ujjvala Krishna is a climate and urban researcher drawn to the intersections of people, policy, and the built environment. Believing that cities are simultaneously wonderful and disastrously ad-hoc, her work explores the friction and negotiations between the various systems that shape our urban spaces. As an architect by training, she is perpetually asking, "how did this really get built?" - seeking to bridge the gap between the chaotic, lived reality of the city, and what shapes it.
Radha Patkar is a visual communication designer from Mumbai, currently based in Bangalore. She works across illustration, comics, and visual storytelling, creating stories inspired by everyday life. Her work draws from the people, places, and small moments that often go unnoticed, turning them into visual narratives that feel familiar, observant, and human.
Nithya Gowda is an urban planner and community engagement specialist. She has worked at the intersection of mobility, climate resilience, urban ecology, and grassroots engagement. Her work consistently turns community knowledge into planning evidence: from leading walkability audits across 300 km of Bengaluru's footpaths to co-authoring a nationally replicable safe routes to school guidebook. Fluent in Kannada, she engages directly with communities that planning processes most often leave out.

name: ALT EFF Film Club x Atta Galatta - Short films we love, followed by an impromptu open mic on the environment.
description:
We are in penultimate screening of the ALT EFF Film Club, where we have been able to bring together people who care deeply about cinema and the environment, and who are seeking spaces to connect with like-minded individuals in Bangalore.
For our July edition, we present a curated short-film programme on 17th  July 7 PM, bringing together three films that explore environmental storytelling through distinct and compelling lenses.
Line-up: 
Would You Still Love Me If I was a Sticky Frog?
Papillon
Shera
Following the screening, ALT EFF Voices invites audience members into an open mic format to share brief, personal responses to environmental prompts, creating a space for reflection, conversation, and exchange.

name: Humzubaan - Hindi and Urdu Poetry Meet
description:
Humzubaan, conceived by Siddharth Surana is an effort to bring together Hindi/Urdu literature enthusiasts together to celebrate the beauty of poetry. The next meet up is happening on Friday, 17th July, 7 PM at Atta Galatta, Indiranagar.
Recite your own or your favourite author’s poems or simply soak in the magic of poetry. Click Here to register as audience or performer. Performance Submissions are open till 12th July.
We will inform you if your poem is selected for the meet. Even if your poem is not selected, you’re most welcome to attend as an audience.
Please note:
1. The poems can be your own original work or of another author. You need to mention clearly and send in your entry in the above-mentioned form.
2. Poems/songs should be in Hindi/Urdu only.
3. If you’re planning to perform with music/props, please mention in the form. You would need to arrange for the instruments and set-up at the venue.
About the host:
Siddharth is founder of AgriCentral, a digital platform serving 11 million farmers. He writes in Hindi, Urdu and English-mostly poems, short stories and essays but with long breaks. He is also an avid quizzer, trekker and a national-level gold medallist archer..

name: Round Table of Stories: The Revival Edit - Storytelling  and Creative Visualisation
description:
The Liminal Space brings to you The Round Table of Stories: The Revival Edit. 
Join in for an afternoon of sharing through storytelling and Creative Visualisation Tools. Ritu and Anupa are highly experienced Storytellers and Facilitators who are Mental health practitioners as well. They come with 25+ years of experience in the related field, and they will take you on a journey of deep self-discovery to help you Create, Learn, and Grow.
Message 9900021372 to register

name: Unhurried Afternoon - Sip and Paint Experience
description: An Unhurried Afternoon is a beginner-friendly sip-and-paint experience designed for those looking to slow down and spend a creative afternoon away from the rush of everyday life. Over two relaxed hours, participants will create a cozy café-inspired acrylic painting featuring a sunflower and blue coffee mug while enjoying a beverage and good company. No prior painting experience is required. All materials are provided, and participants will be guided step-by-step through the process. Ticket includes painting materials and one beverage

name: Taishogoto to Bulbul Tarang - Informative Session and Musical Perfomance
description: An informative session for young minds on the Genesis and Evolution of the Bulbul Tarang, featuring a live demonstration and exhibition and a concert.  Experience the sounds, stories, and evolution of Bulbul Tarang  bridging the past and present.  Join us for an unforgettable evening celebrating the timeless charm and global journey of this remarkable instrument.

name: Magnet and Keychain Making Workshop - Art Workshop
description:
Join me for a creative workshop of magnet and keychain making using acrylic paints.
This is a beginner-friendly session where you can explore your artistic talent and express them in the form of unique and vibrant keychains and magnets that capture your vivid imagination. Personalised art pieces to take home that would be an expression of your inner self!
What’s provided
All the materials will be provided, along with expert guidance
What you need to bring
Just bring your creative vibes along

name: Meet the Author: Indrani Talukdar - Book Discussion
description:
What happens when love becomes a matter of life and death?
Join award-winning author Indrani Talukdar in conversation with Reena Pereira as they discuss Till the Dust Settles, a compelling collection of fiction that moves between danger, humour, and the complexities of everyday life in India.
At the heart of the book is a gripping novelette about a couple forced to flee their rural community after an illicit romance puts them in mortal danger. Alongside it are witty and insightful stories of small-town India—featuring eccentric dowry negotiations, family dramas, unexpected acts of courage, and moments of quiet transformation.
Drawing from her experiences across India and abroad, Talukdar's writing explores the tensions between tradition and change, individual desire and social expectation, while capturing the humour, contradictions, and resilience of ordinary lives.
Whether you're a lover of fiction, curious about contemporary Indian storytelling, or simply looking for an engaging literary evening, this is an opportunity to meet the author, hear about her writing journey, and participate in a lively discussion followed by audience Q&A.
Author: Indrani Talukdar
Moderator: Reena Pereira
Date: Sunday, 19 July 2026
Time: 5:30 PM – 6:30 PM
Venue: Atta Galatta, 178, 5th Main, 9th Cross, First Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru

name: Food In Literature - Book Club
description:
Borders are more than lines on a map; they are stories of power, ambition, and the lives they reshape. This month, we journey through the forgotten history of The Great Hedge of India, uncovering an extraordinary colonial experiment that sought to control trade and people through an immense living barrier. What does it mean to rediscover the hidden landscapes of history and the legacies they leave behind?
 
Book of the month: The Great Hedge of India by Roy Moxham
When: Saturday, 25th July, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Hosted by: Prachi Grover
 
Registration is mandatory as we have limited spots.
 
The session is free & copies of the book are available at the store. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a community for people who love to read and eat.

name: This Isn't S*xy -
description:
Some ambitions are loud.
Others quietly follow you around for years.
The business.
The novel.
The healthier body.
The career change.
The city you want to move to.
The person you suspect you're capable of becoming.
And sometimes...
You don't even know what that thing is yet.
You just know your current life doesn't feel fully yours.
This Isn't S*xy isn't about motivation.
It's about sitting down for two uninterrupted hours to honestly ask yourself what you want—and building a system that makes it much harder to ignore once you know.
We'll map your priorities, challenge vague ideas, translate them into concrete actions, and leave with a personalized operating system for the months ahead.
No productivity gurus.
No life hacks.
No vision boards.
Just thoughtful conversations, paper, pencils, and a room full of people trying to build lives they're excited to wake up to.

name: Divya Leelamritham - Divine stories through Bharatanatyam
description:
Experience an enchanting evening of Bharatanatyam as timeless stories of Ganesha, Rama, Krishna, and Devi come alive through dance, music, and expression. A celebration of devotion, grace, and the joy of surrendering to the Divine, Divya Leelamritham invites audiences of all ages to immerse themselves in the beauty of India's rich cultural heritage.
Join us for an evening filled with devotion, storytelling, rhythm, and the timeless beauty of Bharatanatyam.

name: Queer Quiet Hours - Meetup
description:
Queer Quiet Hours returns for August 2026!
Register here - http://bit.ly/adhdqueeple
 
A soft little corner for LGBTQIA+ folks to slow down, make art, read, journal, daydream, or simply exist in shared company.
 
There’s no pressure to interact. Come with your own project, or just arrive as you are. We’ll also have art materials around for anyone who wants to use them.
 
✨ This space is for LGBTQIA+ folks only. We haven’t opened it up to allies yet. Thank you for understanding.
 
🆓 Free to join, but spots are limited. Link in bio.
 
📅  9 AUGUST, 2026
⏰ Sundays, 2 - 5 PM

name: Curious Minds Trivia Night  - Trivia Night
description:
Forget everything you think a trivia night is. 
Curious Minds Trivia Night is an interactive, people-first experience that blends pop culture, teamwork, strategy, and friendly competition into one memorable evening. Before the event, participants fill out a short registration form sharing their favourite movies, TV shows, music, and interests. Parts of the trivia are then built around those responses, making every edition unique and tailored to the people in the room. Expect a mix of visual rounds, quote challenges, picture rounds, strategic betting rounds, and a few surprises along the way. Some rounds will test your memory, others your observation, and a few will reward teamwork, creativity, and quick thinking.
Whether you’re a seasoned trivia enthusiast or someone looking for a genuinely good evening with strangers — this event is designed to bring people together through laughter, conversation, and a little healthy rivalry.
Come with a team or walk in solo — we’ll find you one. All you need is curiosity and a willingness to play.
Come once. Come back forever

name: TechnoCats - Film Screening
description:
A search for purpose in the fiercely competitive world of the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT).
SYNOPSIS 
Seven young people from seven parts of the country (the Seven Samurai) dream of joining the IIT. Competing against one million applicants, they gain admission, but are soon disillusioned. Will they make their presence felt on the world stage? Or burn out along the way? The filmmaker, an IIT alumnus, follows the Seven Samurai over seven years—from their first semester to the third year of their careers. Interwoven with their journey are four of the filmmaker’s classmates from 35 years ago, including one who made an audiovisual on IIT student life. As he rediscovers old slides and struggles to sync them with the audio track, this forgotten past frames the film’s exploration of winning at all cost. 
DIRECTOR’S STATEMENT 
I enrolled for a B.Tech. in electrical engineering at IIT Delhi, but was dejected at the barrage of tests and emphasis on grades over all else. Where was the time to learn, ponder, speculate? 35 years later, I was surprised to meet students from very different backgrounds who felt similarly. I decided to revisit my past and connect with some of my classmates. Their stories are interwoven with  those of the seven students at the heart of this film, to create a conversation across generations.
